article_title: OpenAI Models and Codex Land on Amazon Bedrock as Microsoft Exclusivity Ends, Reshaping the Cloud-AI Map

content_preview.title: OpenAI Models and Codex Land on Amazon Bedrock as Microsoft Exclusivity Ends, Reshaping the Cloud-AI Map
content_preview.summary: AWS launched OpenAI's frontier models, Codex, and Bedrock Managed Agents in limited preview on April 28, 2026, one day after Microsoft's seven-year exclusivity over OpenAI's IP was rewritten to a non-exclusive license through 2032.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Amazon Web Services and OpenAI announced an expanded partnership on April 28, 2026, bringing OpenAI's frontier models, the Codex coding agent, and a new Amazon Bedrock Managed Agents service into limited preview on AWS, [according to AWS's announcement](https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/aws/bedrock-openai-models). The launch arrived one day after OpenAI and Microsoft restructured their exclusive cloud arrangement, ending a seven-year lock-in that had kept OpenAI's products tied to Az...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Both round-1 findings cleanly addressed. The bot also caught a verbatim-quote error on the Liguori line and corrected it.

round_1_disposition.finding_1_codex_4m_users: Bot accepted finding. The 'four million weekly users' attribution to AWS has been removed entirely. The Codex bullet now describes only what source-0 actually contains (CLI, desktop app, VS Code extension, auth/inference through Bedrock). Clean fix.
round_1_disposition.finding_2_model_versions_caveat: Bot rewrote the 'What We Don't Know' bullet. The new text reads: 'AWS's event recap names GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.4 as the OpenAI models initially available in preview, per the AWS announcements page, but the partnership-announcement and What's New posts use the generic OpenAI frontier models framing without confirming whether other versions or future releases are part of the limited preview.' Verified against source-2: the AWS top-announcements page literally says 'The latest OpenAI models, including GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.4, will be available in preview on Amazon Bedrock.' The new framing is accurate and acknowledges both named versions.
round_1_disposition.finding_3_liguori_attribution: Bot accepted optional finding. Replaced 'an AWS executive' with 'Anthony Liguori, vice president and distinguished engineer at Amazon' (verbatim from source-4). Also corrected the quote's leading clause from 'We had nothing eight weeks ago' to the verbatim 'It's wild to be thinking that we had nothing eight weeks ago' — a precision improvement the reviewer didn't catch.

editor_notes.source_verification: Re-using round-1 verification for unchanged content. Round-2 verifications: GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.4 named together in source-2 verbatim ('The latest OpenAI models, including GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.4, will be available in preview on Amazon Bedrock'). Liguori's full quote 'It's wild to be thinking that we had nothing eight weeks ago, and now we're onstage talking about all of the things we are building together' verified verbatim in source-4, attributed to 'Anthony Liguori, vice president and distinguished engineer at Amazon'.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims now verified. The fabricated 4M-users figure is removed; the model-versions caveat is corrected; the Liguori attribution is precise.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Surgical fixes applied; bot also tightened the Liguori quote beyond what the reviewer flagged.
